visual_notes: Unmistakable copper-red when fresh. Slow oxidation spectrum from red through brown and black to vivid blue-green patina is one of nature's most beautiful chemical sequences. Keweenaw Peninsula specimens show exceptional arborescent habits.
prompt_fragments: copper-red arborescent mass with blue-green malachite bloom
wiry dendritic copper on basalt matrix
warm metallic red fading to verdigris teal
aesthetic_roles: tonal contrast with blue-green secondary minerals
vibe_notes: Copper is the first chapter of human metallurgy — warm, workable, transformative. Its slow oxidation into blues and greens is one of nature's most beautiful chemical reactions.
folklore_summary: The word 'copper' derives from Latin 'cuprum', itself from Greek 'Kuprios' meaning 'of Cyprus', where copper was extensively mined in antiquity.
